title: Tarenna loheri
taxonomy: Tarenna loheri is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family is part of the order Gentianales, and the class Magnoliopsida. The genus Tarenna is a part of the tribe Spermaeae, which includes various species of flowering plants native to tropical regions. Tarenna loheri is a species of flowering plant that has been identified and classified based on its unique characteristics and morphology. Further classification may involve sub-classification into smaller categories, such as order, family, genus, species, and subspecies.
uses: Tarenna loheri is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. It has been traditionally used in various cultures to treat fever and inflammation. intro: Tarenna loheri, a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, is native to the tropical regions of Asia and is commonly known as Loher's Tarenna or Loher's Tarenna. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, with reported health benefits including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Tarenna loheri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Tarenna loheri include teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as dried extracts and tinctures used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
